contentBlocks.original:
कल्याणवृष्टिभिरिवामृतपूरिताभि-
-र्लक्ष्मीस्वयंवरणमङ्गलदीपिकाभिः ।
सेवाभिरम्ब तव पादसरोजमूले
नाकारि किं मनसि भाग्यवतां जनानाम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalyāṇavṛṣṭibhirivāmṛtapūritābhi-
-rlakṣmīsvayaṃvaraṇamaṅgaladīpikābhiḥ ।
sēvābhiramba tava pādasarōjamūlē
nākāri kiṃ manasi bhāgyavatāṃ janānām ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O mother Ambā — through services that are like showers of auspiciousness, that are as if filled with the nectar of the auspicious lamp of Lakṣmī's self-choice ceremony, offered at the root of your lotus-feet — may I know a state of complete fullness.
contentBlocks.meanings: kalyāṇa-vṛṣṭibhiḥ: like showers of auspiciousness | amṛta-pūritābhiḥ: as if filled with nectar | lakṣmī-svayaṃvaraṇa-maṅgala-dīpikābhiḥ: auspicious lamp of Lakṣmī's self-choice ceremony | sevābhiḥ: through services | amba: O mother | tava pāda-saroja-mūle: at the root of your lotus-feet | pūrṇatā: complete fullness

contentBlocks.original:
एतावदेव जननि स्पृहणीयमास्ते
त्वद्वन्दनेषु सलिलस्थगिते च नेत्रे ।
सान्निध्यमुद्यदरुणायुतसोदरस्य
त्वद्विग्रहस्य परया सुधयाप्लुतस्य ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ētāvadēva janani spṛhaṇīyamāstē
tvadvandanēṣu salilasthagitē cha nētrē ।
sānnidhyamudyadaruṇāyutasōdarasya
tvadvigrahasya parayā sudhayāplutasya ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Only this much is to be desired, O mother — that in [acts of] bowing to you, the eyes are veiled by tears — and the proximity of one who is like a sibling to ten thousand rising suns is attained.
contentBlocks.meanings: etāvad eva: only this much | spṛhaṇīyam āste: is to be desired | tvad-vandaneṣu: in acts of bowing to you | salila-sthagite: veiled by tears | netre: eyes | sānnidhyam: proximity | udyat-aruṇa-āyuta: like ten thousand rising suns | sodara: sibling/like a sibling

contentBlocks.original:
ईशत्वनामकलुषाः कति वा न सन्ति
ब्रह्मादयः प्रतिभवं प्रलयाभिभूताः ।
एकः स एव जननि स्थिरसिद्धिरास्ते
यः पादयोस्तव सकृत्प्रणतिं करोति ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
īśatvanāmakaluṣāḥ kati vā na santi
brahmādayaḥ pratibhavaṃ pralayābhibhūtāḥ ।
ēkaḥ sa ēva janani sthirasiddhirāstē
yaḥ pādayōstava sakṛtpraṇatiṃ karōti ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O mother — how many are there who bear the name of sovereignty, like Brahmā and others, who are repeatedly overcome by dissolution? One alone, O mother, has stable attainment — and that one is he who has your grace.
contentBlocks.meanings: īśatva-nāma-kaluṣāḥ: who bear the taint/burden of the name of sovereignty | kati vā na santi: how many there are | brahma-ādayaḥ: Brahmā and others | prati-bhavam: in every cycle/birth | pralaya-abhibhūtāḥ: overcome by dissolution | ekaḥ saḥ eva: that one alone | sthira-siddhi: stable attainment | āste: has | yasya tvad-anugrahaḥ: who has your grace

contentBlocks.original:
लब्ध्वा सकृत्त्रिपुरसुन्दरि तावकीनं
कारुण्यकन्दलितकान्तिभरं कटाक्षम् ।
कन्दर्पकोटिसुभगास्त्वयि भक्तिभाजः
सम्मोहयन्ति तरुणीर्भुवनत्रयेऽपि ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
labdhvā sakṛttripurasundari tāvakīnaṃ
kāruṇyakandalitakāntibharaṃ kaṭākṣam ।
kandarpakōṭisubhagāstvayi bhaktibhājaḥ
sammōhayanti taruṇīrbhuvanatrayē'pi ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Tripura Sundarī — having once obtained your sidelong glance, filled with the beauty of compassion in full bloom — those who are devoted to you become beautiful as ten million Kāmas, and they are absorbed in you, no longer seeking anything else.
contentBlocks.meanings: labdhvā sakṛt: having once obtained | tripura-sundari: O Tripura Sundarī | tāvakīnam: yours | kāruṇya-kandalita-kānti-bharam: filled with the beauty of compassion in full bloom | kaṭākṣam: sidelong glance | kandarpа-koṭi-subhagāḥ: beautiful as ten million Kāmas | tvayi: in you | ananyāḥ: no longer seeking anything else

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रीं‍कारमेव तव नाम गृणन्ति वेदा
मातस्त्रिकोणनिलये त्रिपुरे त्रिनेत्रे ।
त्वत्संस्मृतौ यमभटाभिभवं विहाय
दीव्यन्ति नन्दनवने सह लोकपालैः ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃ‍kāramēva tava nāma gṛṇanti vēdā
mātastrikōṇanilayē tripurē trinētrē ।
tvatsaṃsmṛtau yamabhaṭābhibhavaṃ vihāya
dīvyanti nandanavanē saha lōkapālaiḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O mother — dwelling in the triangle, O Tripura, O three-eyed one — the Vedas declare Hrīṃ alone to be your name. In [moments of] remembering you, the messengers of Yama who have come to seize [a devotee] turn away — astonished, vanquished.
contentBlocks.meanings: hrīṃkāram eva: Hrīṃ alone | tava nāma: your name | gṛṇanti vedāḥ: the Vedas declare | trikona-nilaye: dwelling in the triangle | tripure: O Tripura | tri-netre: O three-eyed one | tvat-saṃsmṛtau: in remembering you | yama-bhaṭāḥ: messengers of Yama (Death) | āgata: who have come | palāyanam: turn away | vismita: astonished

contentBlocks.original:
हन्तुः पुरामधिगलं परिपीयमानः
क्रूरः कथं न भविता गरलस्य वेगः ।
नाश्वासनाय यदि मातरिदं तवार्थं
देहस्य शश्वदमृताप्लुतशीतलस्य ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hantuḥ purāmadhigalaṃ paripīyamānaḥ
krūraḥ kathaṃ na bhavitā garalasya vēgaḥ ।
nāśvāsanāya yadi mātaridaṃ tavārthaṃ
dēhasya śaśvadamṛtāplutaśītalasya ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: How could the poison's power not be fierce and cruel — being drunk down across the throat of the destroyer of the three cities [Śiva]? Unless, O mother, it was for the purpose of reassuring [the world] — 'This is meant to symbolize the protection offered by your [grace].'
contentBlocks.meanings: hantuḥ pura-ādhi-galam: across the throat of the destroyer of the three cities (Śiva) | paripīyamānaḥ: being drunk/consumed | krūraḥ: fierce/cruel | kathaṃ na bhaviṭā: how would it not be | garalasya vegaḥ: the force of the poison | āśvāsanāya: for the purpose of reassuring | tava artham: this symbolizes your [protection]

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वज्ञतां सदसि वाक्पटुतां प्रसूते
देवि त्वदङ्घ्रिसरसीरुहयोः प्रणामः ।
किं च स्फुरन्मकुटमुज्ज्वलमातपत्रं
द्वे चामरे च महतीं वसुधां ददाति ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvajñatāṃ sadasi vākpaṭutāṃ prasūtē
dēvi tvadaṅghrisarasīruhayōḥ praṇāmaḥ ।
kiṃ cha sphuranmakuṭamujjvalamātapatraṃ
dvē chāmarē cha mahatīṃ vasudhāṃ dadāti ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Goddess — prostration at your lotus-feet produces omniscience and eloquence in the assembly. Moreover — a shining crown above, and an upward-pointing royal parasol, are [also obtained] by the devoted worshipper.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarva-jñatāṃ: omniscience | sadasi: in the assembly | vāk-paṭutāṃ: eloquence | prasūte: produces | devī tvad-aṅghri-sarasīruhayoḥ: O Goddess — at your two lotus-feet | praṇāmaḥ: prostration | sphurat-makuṭam: a shining crown above | ūrdhva-udañcat-rāja-chatram: a royal parasol pointing upward | kim ca: moreover

contentBlocks.original:
कल्पद्रुमैरभिमतप्रतिपादनेषु
कारुण्यवारिधिभिरम्ब भवात्कटाक्षैः ।
आलोकय त्रिपुरसुन्दरि मामनाथं
त्वय्येव भक्तिभरितं त्वयि बद्धतृष्णम् ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalpadrumairabhimatapratipādanēṣu
kāruṇyavāridhibhiramba bhavātkaṭākṣaiḥ ।
ālōkaya tripurasundari māmanāthaṃ
tvayyēva bhaktibharitaṃ tvayi baddhatṛṣṇam ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Tripura Sundarī — you are an ocean of compassion (kāruṇya-vāridhi), like the wish-fulfilling trees (kalpa-drumas) in satisfying all desired things. Look upon me, the unprotected one, with those eyes that are like blooming, shining wish-fulfilling blossoms.
contentBlocks.meanings: kalpa-drumaiḥ: like wish-fulfilling trees | abhimata-pratipādaneṣu: in satisfying all desired things | kāruṇya-vāridhibhiḥ: [you who are] an ocean of compassion | amba: O mother | bhavat-kaṭākṣaiḥ: with your sidelong glances | ālokaya: look upon | tripura-sundari: O Tripura Sundarī | mām anātham: me, the unprotected/shelterless one

contentBlocks.original:
हन्तेतरेष्वपि मनांसि निधाय चान्ये
भक्तिं वहन्ति किल पामरदैवतेषु ।
त्वामेव देवि मनसा समनुस्मरामि
त्वामेव नौमि शरणं जननि त्वमेव ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hantētarēṣvapi manāṃsi nidhāya chānyē
bhaktiṃ vahanti kila pāmaradaivatēṣu ।
tvāmēva dēvi manasā samanusmarāmi
tvāmēva naumi śaraṇaṃ janani tvamēva ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Alas — others too place their minds elsewhere and yet carry devotion to wretched deities. We, however, remember only you, O Goddess — and we are not deviated by anyone. Yet — when will you turn your face to us?
contentBlocks.meanings: hanta: alas | itareṣv api: to others as well | manāṃsi nidhāya: placing their minds | bhaktiṃ vahanti: carry devotion | pāmara-daivatеṣu: to wretched/low deities | tvāmeva devī: only you, O Goddess | manasā samanusmrāmaḥ: we remember with the mind | anyena: by anyone | na vicālitāḥ: not deviated | kadā: when | mukhaṃ vaḥ: your face toward us

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्येषु सत्स्वपि कटाक्षनिरीक्षणाना-
-मालोकय त्रिपुरसुन्दरि मां कदाचित् ।
नूनं मया तु सदृशः करुणैकपात्रं
जातो जनिष्यति जनो न च जायते वा ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣyēṣu satsvapi kaṭākṣanirīkṣaṇānā-
-mālōkaya tripurasundari māṃ kadāchit ।
nūnaṃ mayā tu sadṛśaḥ karuṇaikapātraṃ
jātō janiṣyati janō na cha jāyatē vā ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Tripura Sundarī — even though there are other worthy targets for your sidelong glance — do look upon me at some point. Certainly there is no one like me who is more negligent — and therefore it is all the more [urgent for you to look upon me].
contentBlocks.meanings: lakṣyeṣu satsu api: even though there are worthy targets | kaṭākṣa-nirīkṣaṇānām: for the sidelong glances | ālokaya: do look upon | tripura-sundari: O Tripura Sundarī | māṃ kadācit: me at some point | nūnam: certainly | mayā tu sadṛśaḥ: one like me | kaḥ: who | adhikaḥ pramādī: more negligent/worse off

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रीं ह्रीमिति प्रतिदिनं जपतां तवाख्यां
किं नाम दुर्लभमिह त्रिपुराधिवासे ।
मालाकिरीटमदवारणमाननीया
तान्सेवते वसुमती स्वयमेव लक्ष्मीः ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃ hrīmiti pratidinaṃ japatāṃ tavākhyāṃ
kiṃ nāma durlabhamiha tripurādhivāsē ।
mālākirīṭamadavāraṇamānanīyā
tānsēvatē vasumatī svayamēva lakṣmīḥ ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Tripurā, dweller of Tripura — for those who daily chant your name 'Hrīṃ Hrīṃ' — what is there in this world that is unattainable? Garlands, crowns, intoxicated elephants, honor — all are obtained.
contentBlocks.meanings: hrīṃ hrīṃ iti: chanting 'Hrīṃ Hrīṃ' | pratidinam: daily | japataṃ: for those who chant | tavākhyām: your name | kim nāma durlabham: what is unattainable? | tripura-adhivāse: O dweller of Tripura | mālā: garlands | kirīṭa: crown | mada-vāraṇa: intoxicated elephant | māna: honor

contentBlocks.original:
सम्पत्कराणि सकलेन्द्रियनन्दनानि
साम्राज्यदाननिरतानि सरोरुहाक्षि ।
त्वद्वन्दनानि दुरिताहरणोद्यतानि
मामेव मातरनिशं कलयन्तु नान्यम् ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sampatkarāṇi sakalēndriyanandanāni
sāmrājyadānaniratāni sarōruhākṣi ।
tvadvandanāni duritāharaṇōdyatāni
māmēva mātaraniśaṃ kalayantu nānyam ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O lotus-eyed one — the acts of bowing to you make all the senses rejoice, bring prosperity, are devoted to granting sovereignty, and are capable — readily — of destroying what is sinful. Such are the services at your lotus-feet.
contentBlocks.meanings: sampat-karāṇi: prosperity-bringing | sakala-indriya-nandanāni: making all the senses rejoice | sāmrājya-dāna-niratāni: devoted to granting sovereignty | sara-ruha-akṣi: O lotus-eyed one | tvad-vandanāni: acts of bowing to you | durita-āharaṇa-udyatāni: capable of destroying what is sinful | su-karāṇi: easily done, readily accessible

contentBlocks.original:
कल्पोपसंहृतिषु कल्पितताण्डवस्य
देवस्य खण्डपरशोः परभैरवस्य ।
पाशाङ्कुशैक्षवशरासनपुष्पबाणा
सा साक्षिणी विजयते तव मूर्तिरेका ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalpōpasaṃhṛtiṣu kalpitatāṇḍavasya
dēvasya khaṇḍaparaśōḥ parabhairavasya ।
pāśāṅkuśaikṣavaśarāsanapuṣpabāṇā
sā sākṣiṇī vijayatē tava mūrtirēkā ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: At the dissolution of aeons, she who is the consort of the one who has begun the tāṇḍava dance — the one wielding the partial axe (Śiva) — the supreme Bhairavī — she who holds the noose, the goad, the sugar-cane bow, and the flower-arrow — that Pārvatī ever dwells in my heart.
contentBlocks.meanings: kalpa-upasaṃhṛtiṣu: at the dissolution of aeons | kalpita-tāṇḍavasya: of the one who has begun the tāṇḍava dance | devasya: of the god | khaṇḍa-paraśoḥ: wielding the partial axe | para-bhairavī: the supreme Bhairavī | pāśa: noose | aṅkuśa: goad | ikṣava-śara-āsana: sugar-cane bow | puṣpa-bāṇā: flower-arrow | sā: that | pārvatī: Pārvatī | lagnam: dwelling

contentBlocks.original:
लग्नं सदा भवतु मातरिदं तवार्धं
तेजः परं बहुलकुङ्कुमपङ्कशोणम् ।
भास्वत्किरीटममृतांशुकलावतंसं
मध्ये त्रिकोणनिलयं परमामृतार्द्रम् ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lagnaṃ sadā bhavatu mātaridaṃ tavārdhaṃ
tējaḥ paraṃ bahulakuṅkumapaṅkaśōṇam ।
bhāsvatkirīṭamamṛtāṃśukalāvataṃsaṃ
madhyē trikōṇanilayaṃ paramāmṛtārdram ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: May this — your half, O mother — always remain attached to [my mind]. That supreme radiance, red as abundant vermilion paste — bearing the blazing crown — adorned with the moon-crescent — the dwelling-place of supreme nectar.
contentBlocks.meanings: lagnam sadā bhavatu: may it always remain attached | mātaḥ: O mother | idam tava ardham: this your half [of the divine form] | tejas param: supreme radiance | bahula-kuṅkuma-paṅka-śoṇam: red as abundant vermilion paste | bhāsvat-kirīṭam: bearing the blazing crown | amṛta-aṃśu-kalā-vatamsam: adorned with the moon-crescent of nectar

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रीं‍कारमेव तव नाम तदेव रूपं
त्वन्नाम दुर्लभमिह त्रिपुरे गृणन्ति ।
त्वत्तेजसा परिणतं वियदादिभूतं
सौख्यं तनोति सरसीरुहसम्भवादेः ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃ‍kāramēva tava nāma tadēva rūpaṃ
tvannāma durlabhamiha tripurē gṛṇanti ।
tvattējasā pariṇataṃ viyadādibhūtaṃ
saukhyaṃ tanōti sarasīruhasambhavādēḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O Tripurā — Hrīṃ alone is your name; that alone is your form. In this [world], those who cannot [find any other way] chant your unattainable name as Tripura. Through your radiance, the universe beginning from the sky has evolved — and so it is chanted as your name.
contentBlocks.meanings: hrīṃkāram eva: Hrīṃ alone | tava nāmam: your name | tad eva rūpam: that alone is your form | tvam nāma durlabham: your unattainable name | tripure: O Tripurā | gṛṇanti: chant | tvat-tejasā: through your radiance | pariṇatam: evolved/transformed | viyad-ādibhūtam: universe beginning from the sky (ether, the first element)

contentBlocks.original:
ह्रीं‍कारत्रयसम्पुटेन महता मन्त्रेण सन्दीपितं
स्तोत्रं यः प्रतिवासरं तव पुरो मातर्जपेन्मन्त्रवित् ।
तस्य क्षोणिभुजो भवन्ति वशगा लक्ष्मीश्चिरस्थायिनी
वाणी निर्मलसूक्तिभारभारिता जागर्ति दीर्घं वयः ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
hrīṃ‍kāratrayasampuṭēna mahatā mantrēṇa sandīpitaṃ
stōtraṃ yaḥ prativāsaraṃ tava purō mātarjapēnmantravit ।
tasya kṣōṇibhujō bhavanti vaśagā lakṣmīśchirasthāyinī
vāṇī nirmalasūktibhārabhāritā jāgarti dīrghaṃ vayaḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: This hymn — ignited by the great mantra of three Hrīṃkāras combined — whoever recites it daily before you, O mother, with the knowledge of mantra — he becomes one whose speech is Sarasvatī herself — and reaches the state of the most fortunate. This is the phalaśruti of the Kalyāṇa Vṛṣṭi Stavam.
contentBlocks.meanings: hrīṃkāra-traya-sampuṭena: by the great mantra of three Hrīṃkāras combined | mahata mantrena: by the great mantra | sandīpitam: ignited | stotram: this hymn | yaḥ prativāsaram: whoever daily | tava puraḥ: before you | japet mantra-vit: recites knowing the mantra | mātaḥ: O mother | vāgīśvarī: Sarasvatī, goddess of speech | mukha-padmam: lotus-mouth | tattvata: truly | prapadyate: attains/reaches
